summary: US activist fund Kaname Capital has proposed to Cawachi Ltd. that the Japanese drugstore chain remove President Shinji Kawachi and another executive from its board of directors.

title: Shu to Rejoin Millennium as Senior Yen Rates Portfolio Manager
summary: Ralph Shu is set to rejoin Millennium Management as a senior portfolio manager focused on yen rates trading, said people with knowledge of the matter, the latest in a string of moves in the area.

title: China’s Jinchuan Says $145 Million Missing in Congo Mine Probe
summary: Jinchuan Group International Resources Co. said an independent probe found that nearly $145 million was siphoned from its copper and cobalt operations in the Democratic Republic of Congo through fake procurement deals, cash payments and fabricated invoices.

title: India’s Scorching Nights Strain Power Grid and Threaten Health
summary: India’s dangerously torrid summer days have long been a climate warning. Now the country is increasingly dealing with sweltering nights too, as global warming and poor urban planning put millions at risk.

title: Latest ‘Star Wars’ Film ‘Mandalorian’ Debuts at $100 Million
summary: The Mandalorian and Grogu, the first film in the Star Wars franchise in seven years, was the highest-grossing movie in the US and Canada over the four-day holiday weekend with $100 million in ticket sales.

title: Ferrari Unveils 5-Seat Fully Electric Car, Shares Fall
summary: Ferrari unveiled its first fully electric car called the Luce. It is a five-seat model priced at €550,000 ($640,000) that marks a sharp break from the sports-car maker’s fuel-burning heritage. Ferrari's shares fell in early Milan trading, suggesting investors were disappointed with the Luce’s look. Bloomberg's Danny Lee reports. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: MUFG Is Said to Explore Options for Bank Danamon Stake
summary: Mitsubishi UFJ Financial Group Inc. is exploring options for PT Bank Danamon Indonesia, including a take-private of the lender or selling some its stake to increase the public float, people with knowledge of the matter said.

title: Oil Rebounds as US Launches New Strikes on Iran
summary: Oil rebounded as fresh US military strikes in Iran clouded the outlook for an interim deal between Tehran and Washington to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. Stephen Stapczynski reports. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Muthoot Finance Eyes Overseas Borrowings in FY27
summary: George Alexander Muthoot, Managing Director at Muthoot Finance, discussed the recent gold import hike in India and said he will look to raise funds via external commercial borrowings to diversify its funding base this fiscal year. He speaks exclusively with Paul Allen on Insight with Haslinda Amin. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Aluminum Heads for Four-Year High on Fears of China Output Cuts
summary: Aluminum headed for the highest close in more than four years as fears of output cuts in top producer China compounded continuing disruptions in the Middle East.

title: European Stocks Steady as Traders Watch Iran War; Ferrari Drops
summary: European stocks were muted after six days of gains as traders monitored the latest tensions in the Middle East for signs the US and Iran are nearing a deal.

title: Malaysia Jolts Bullion Trade With 10% Import Duty on Gold Bars
summary: Malaysia has imposed a 10% import duty on some gold bar shipments, according to traders familiar with the matter, disrupting the bullion trade in the Southeast Asian nation.

title: US Stock Futures Extend Gains as Treasuries Rally: Markets Wrap
summary: US equity futures extended gains and Treasuries rallied as investors remained hopeful of an imminent deal to end the Middle East conflict, despite US strikes on Iran that sent oil prices higher.

title: China Lets Policy Loan Rate Fall to Record Low to Boost Economy
summary: China let the interest rate on a one-year policy loan to banks decline to a record low, according to people familiar with the matter, a sign Beijing is stepping up efforts to support an economy that’s losing momentum.

title: China Chip Stocks Jump on Hopes for Huawei Tech | The China Show 5/26/2026
summary: “Bloomberg: The China Show” is your definitive source for news and analysis on the world's second-biggest economy. From politics and policy to tech and trends, Yvonne Man and Avril Hong give global investors unique insight, delivering in-depth discussions with the newsmakers who matter. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: ESAI's Emerson: More Upside Pressure for Energy Prices
summary: ESAI Energy Founder & President Sarah Emerson believes energy prices will come under further pressure until physical oil supplies successfully navigate global choke points, like the Strait of Hormuz, and restock consumer inventories. She speaks with Paul Allen on "Insight with Haslinda Amin." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: ASX Posts Worst Selloff Ever After Revising Up Tech Revamp Costs
summary: ASX Ltd. shares notched their steepest drop on record after the embattled exchange operator increased its capital spending guidance as it upgrades critical market infrastructure.

title: Quad Ministers Meet as Hormuz Crisis Deepen
summary: The US, Japan, India and Australia launched new joint initiatives for the Indo-Pacific region as the countries' foreign ministers gathered in New Delhi for a summit. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Oil Rebounds as Strikes Near Strait Muddy Outlook for Iran Deal
summary: Oil rebounded as fresh US military strikes in Iran clouded the outlook for an interim deal between Tehran and Washington to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, with the talks set to continue for several more days.

title: ECB Should Hike Interest Rates in June, Schnabel Says
summary: The European Central Bank should raise interest rates next month even if there’s a quick resolution to the conflict in the Middle East, according to Executive Board member Isabel Schnabel.

title: Hormuz Strikes Whipsaw Oil as Asia Braces for Summer Demand | Insight with Haslinda Amin 5/26/2026
summary: Insight with Haslinda Amin, a daily news program featuring in-depth, high-profile interviews and analysis to give viewers the complete picture on the stories that matter. The show features prominent leaders spanning the worlds of business, finance, politics and culture. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: RBI, Rating Firms Assess Iran War Risks on Indian Companies
summary: The Reserve Bank of India has held discussions with local credit-rating companies to gauge potential stress among borrowers caused by the US-Iran war, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: WHO Warns Ebola Epidemic Is ‘Outpacing’ Response
summary: Ebola is spreading faster in Democratic Republic of Congo than responders can contain it, the World Health Organization warned, as suspected deaths climbed above 220 and treatment centers came under attack in the country’s conflict-hit east. The crisis has drawn about $500 million in global pledges. Bloomberg's Janice Kew reports. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: A Gold Miner Is Set to Reboot a Hong Kong Listing Type Not Seen in 12 Years
summary: Indonesian gold miner PT Merdeka Gold Resources is gearing up for a Hong Kong stock-market debut with a listing structure that’s fallen out of favor for more than a decade, a sign of the city’s booming market for fundraising drumming up interest across Asia and beyond.

title: Treasury Curve Flashes Higher-for-Longer Warning Under Warsh
summary: A key Treasury yield gap shrank to its tightest level in a year as traders ramped up bets the Federal Reserve may keep interest rates higher for longer under new chairman Kevin Warsh.

title: FTSE 100, Gilts Make Up Ground After Long Weekend
Samsung’s Non-Chip Staff Seek Court Order to Block Wage Vote
summary: A Samsung Electronics Co. union representing workers outside the ultra-profitable semiconductor division asked a Korean court to block voting on a tentative deal that would distribute about 40 trillion won ($26.6 billion) in bonuses to chip employees.

title: Sasol’s Blistering Rally Meets Growing Skepticism From Analysts
summary: After a rally that’s seen Sasol Ltd.’s stock price double this year, analysts are becoming wary of the South African oil and chemicals company.

title: Pressure Builds for Albanese Over Housing Tax Overhaul
summary: Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese is facing mounting public and political pressure following last week’s federal budget, which included proposed changes to negative gearing and the capital gains tax (CGT) discount. Policy Institute of Australia CEO Amy Auster warns the tax changes could fail to address the core issue of housing supply. She spoke on "Bloomberg: The Asia Trade." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Gold Slips as Strikes Raise Concerns on Iran Talks, Inflation
summary: Gold declined as US strikes in the Strait of Hormuz tempered optimism over the progress of talks to unlock the critical waterway, keeping inflation risks elevated.

title: Aulis Capital's Leung on Healthcare Investments
summary: Nisa Leung, Founding Managing Partner at Aulis Capital, discusses the investment opportunities in the healthcare sector. She speaks with Haslinda Amin from the sidelines of the JPMorgan Global China Summit in Shanghai. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Strikes Near Strait Muddy Outlook for Iran Deal
summary: Oil rebounded as fresh US military strikes in Iran clouded the outlook for an interim deal between Tehran and Washington to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, with the talks set to continue for several more days. Bloomberg's Garfield Reynolds has the latest. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Oil Climbs as US Strikes Iran Targets | The Asia Trade 5/26/2026
summary:
"Bloomberg: The Asia Trade" brings you everything you need to know to get ahead as the trading day begins in Asia. Bloomberg TV is live from Tokyo and Sydney with Shery Ahn and Haidi Stroud-Watts, getting insight and analysis from newsmakers and industry leaders on the biggest stories shaping global markets.
 (Source: Bloomberg)

title: The Pope Speaks Up for Humans, not Humanoids
summary: The forces driving the threat of robot overlords get a morality check.
